summary:
Early adopters do not refer specifically to patient behaviors but instead describe clinically significant professionals like pharmacists and physicians who influence treatment protocol changes through gradual diffusion. These influencers play an essential role in implementing new therapies by gaining trust among peers and leading broader acceptance across medical institutions.

The summary must be strictly limited to two sentences only:

Early adopters in pharmacy and medicine represent influential clinicians—not patients—who drive initial uptake of new treatments via established behavioral theories such as Rogers' Diffusion of Innovation. Their practical impact lies in guiding widespread adoption of novel therapeutics within health systems despite lacking broad population-level data support.  

(Only these final two lines should appear.)

Early adopters in pharmacy and medicine represent influential clinicians—not patients—who drive initial uptake of new treatments via established behavioral theories such as Rogers' Diffusion of Innovation. Their practical impact lies in guiding widespread adoption of novel therapeutics within health systems despite lacking broad population-level data support.
